mechanism_narrative: Sustained nonfarm payroll expansion signals corporate hiring strength and aggregate demand growth, which supports earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change vs 20-day forward return remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth is associated with negative forward returns in more than 40% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals imminent labor market weakening and consumer spending slowdown, leading to negative forward S&P 500 returns as recession fears intensify.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window
failure_condition: After UNRATE trigger conditions, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both inflation is elevated and the federal funds rate is high, the combined compression of equity risk premia and discount rates produces a strongly negative signal for near-term equity returns as monetary tightening bites.
candidate_trigger: both CPIAUCSL year-over-year change > 4% and FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY > 4% and FEDFUNDS > 4%, the proportion of positive 20-day forward S&P 500 returns exceeds 50%, or the rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real retail sales growth indicates robust consumer spending and economic momentum, creating a favorable environment for equities as corporate revenues rise.
failure_condition: When RSFS YoY > 5%, the median 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative or not statistically different from zero (bootstrap p > 0.10), or the hit rate of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady industrial production growth reflects expanding factory output and capacity utilization, a broad measure of economic activity that tends to correlate with rising corporate profits and positive equity returns over multi-month horizons.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change vs 60-day forward return remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO changes are associated with negative forward returns in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts decline concurrently with elevated interest rates, it signals a synchronized downturn in the housing market and broader credit-sensitive economic activity, producing negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition holds, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative at the 5% significance level, or the positive return rate exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend GDP growth indicates strong aggregate demand and economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and supports positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After strong GDP growth releases, the 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or more than 40% of such instances result in negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in the CPI index reflect rising inflation that erodes purchasing power and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, leading to negative short-term equity returns as discount rates rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC of CPIAUCSL month-over-month change vs 5-day forward return remains above -0.02 (i.e., fails to show consistent negative correlation) over any 2-year rolling window, or positive CPI prints are followed by positive returns more than 50% of the time.
